fields.case_caption: MARGARET A. COLE and THOMAS B. COLE, Plaintiffs, v. MIDWEST FIDELITY BANK, N.A., Defendant.
fields.court: UNITED STATES DISTRICT COURT FOR THE EASTERN DISTRICT OF WISCONSIN
claims: COUNT I - Violation of the Right to Financial Privacy Act 12 U.S.C. § 3401 et seq.
COUNT II - Negligence — Failure to Comply with Litigation Hold
causes_of_action: COUNT I - Violation of the Right to Financial Privacy Act 12 U.S.C. § 3401 et seq.
COUNT II - Negligence — Failure to Comply with Litigation Hold
relief_sought: Judgment against Defendant for violation of 12 U.S.C. § 3401 et seq.
Statutory damages pursuant to 12 U.S.C. § 3417(a) of not less than $100 per day for each day of violation, up to a maximum of $10,000
Actual damages, including compensation for emotional distress, reputational harm, and loss of privacy
Damages for Defendant's negligent destruction of records subject to the litigation hold
Declaratory relief stating that Defendant's conduct violated the Right to Financial Privacy Act
Preservation order requiring Defendant to retain all remaining records related to Plaintiffs' accounts
Reasonable attorneys' fees and costs pursuant to 12 U.S.C. § 3417(a)

field_snippets.court: UNITED STATES DISTRICT COURT FOR THE EASTERN DISTRICT OF WISCONSIN
field_snippets.plaintiffs: MARGARET A. COLE and THOMAS B. COLE, Plaintiffs,
field_snippets.jurisdiction: This Court has jurisdiction over this action pursuant to 28 U.S.C. § 1331
field_snippets.venue: Venue is proper in this district pursuant to 28 U.S.C. § 1391(b)
